changes_applied.fix: Rewrite description with pipeline position + triggers
changes_applied.details: New description specifies phase 3.5 (between capture and verdict writing), names the 3 evidence types supported, names who invokes it (verdict-writer agent), and gives concrete trigger phrases. Added 2 new YAML triggers: 'review my evidence', 'score the screenshots'.

changes_applied.fix: Promote disable/offline instructions to top of skill
changes_applied.details: The 3 disable methods (env var, config, offline auto-skip) were buried 276+ lines deep in the file. Consolidated into a single 'How to disable' section placed right after 'When to Use'. Users who don't want AI analysis can see how to turn it off without reading the entire protocol.

changes_applied.fix: Add 'Common mistakes' section up front (moved from end + expanded)
changes_applied.details: Audit flagged that anti-patterns lived at the end after 200+ lines of detail. New 'Common mistakes' section placed after 'How to disable' with 4 concrete misuse patterns: treating confidence as verdict, analyzing invalid evidence, findings-list inflation, uncapped CI costs. Gives the model the guardrails early instead of after wading through protocol.

changes_skipped.fix: Split into minimal SKILL.md (<120 lines) + new references/analysis-protocol.md
changes_skipped.reason: The highest-impact audit recommendation — body is legitimately bloated at 354 lines. But splitting requires fabricating a new 200+ line reference file by carefully moving content without breaking semantics. That's substantial content engineering better suited to a focused subagent pass. Bumped priority for follow-up.

changes_skipped.fix: Move evidence classification logic (lines 124-152) into scripts/detect-evidence-type.sh
changes_skipped.reason: Requires writing + testing a shell script with xxd/python JSON detection and safe fallbacks. Out of in-session scope; marked for follow-up when subagent capacity returns.

The schema table is useful for agents consuming AnalysisResult output programmatically. Collapsing to 'see schema.json' moves complexity elsewhere without reducing it. Net negative for an orchestrator-facing skill.
Would require fabricating an end-to-end example from scratch. Better done with real evidence from a test run, which requires subagent capacity.

description_before: AI-augmented evidence review: vision models analyze screenshots, LLMs check API/CLI output. Produces 0-100 confidence scores and findings. Optional in offline mode.
description_after: Use after capturing validation evidence (phase 3.5, between capture and verdict writing) when you want AI to systematically review screenshots, API responses, and CLI output — flagging issues a human reviewer might skim past. Produces a 0-100 confidence score and structured findings per evidence item (3 types supported: screenshot / api-response / cli-output). Optional and easy to disable in offline or cost-sensitive environments. Reach for this on phrases like 'review my evidence', 'score the screenshots', 'AI check the API responses', 'spot issues in the logs', or when the verdict-writer agent needs confidence signals before ruling PASS/FAIL.
self_review_notes: File grew 354->375 because structural fixes (disable + common-mistakes at top) added content without removing any. The big structural refactor — splitting 230+ lines of protocol into a reference — was correctly skipped as too risky for in-session work. Most practical wins: users can now find how to disable without reading the whole file, and the anti-patterns are visible before the reader invests in the protocol. Full restructure tracked as follow-up.
